sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: EOG Resources (EOG) Price Target Raised to $127 at Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Wells Fargo recently raised its price target for EOG Resources (NYSE: EOG) to $127 from $126, maintaining an ‘Overweight’ rating, citing the company's strong capital discipline despite a challenging macro oil environment. This update comes as Susquehanna revised its own price target for EOG downwards to $151 from $161, acknowledging a current supply glut and softer demand in the oil market. Despite short-term pressures, Susquehanna remains bullish on the long-term outlook for natural gas demand driven by data centers and electrification.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Devon Energy (DVN) Price Target by $6
feed.summary: Wells Fargo recently raised its price target for Devon Energy (DVN) from $37 to $43, maintaining an 'Overweight' rating, despite a pressured macro oil environment. Susquehanna also increased its price target to $45, citing a long-term bullish outlook for natural gas even as oil prices face downward pressure. Both firms highlight concerns about global oversupply and softer demand in the oil sector, favoring companies with strong capital discipline.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock ticks up after Chronosphere deal closes as traders eye next earnings
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock saw a modest increase after officially closing its acquisition of observability firm Chronosphere. Investors are now keenly awaiting the company's upcoming earnings report, expected around February 12th, to gain insight into the integration costs, pricing strategy for observability offerings, and the impact on near-term margin outlook. The acquisition aims to integrate operations monitoring with security, aligning with enterprise demands for fewer vendors and deeper partnerships despite entering a competitive observability market.

feed.title: We Expect a More Disciplined Capital Allocation Strategy From Air Products' New Management
feed.summary: This article discusses the expectation of a more disciplined capital allocation strategy from Air Products' new management. It highlights the favorable structure of the industrial gas industry, where long-term contracts and high switching costs contribute to economic moats and predictable cash flow for producers like Air Products. The previous articles mentioned indicate solid earnings despite helium headwinds and ongoing negotiations for a significant ammonia partnership.

feed.title: DTE Energy schedules full year 2025 earnings release, conference call
feed.summary: DTE Energy (NYSE: DTE) has scheduled its full year 2025 earnings release for Tuesday, Feb. 17, 2026, before the market opens. The company will host a conference call on the same day at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss the results. Investors, media, and the public can access a live webcast on dteenergy.com/investors or join via provided dial-in numbers.

feed.title: JPMorgan Downgrades Otis Worldwide to Neutral From Overweight, $98 Price Target
feed.summary: JPMorgan has downgraded Otis Worldwide (OTIS) from Overweight to Neutral, setting a price target of $98. This news follows a series of recent analyst adjustments to Otis Worldwide's price targets and ratings from other firms like Barclays, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, and Wolfe. The company recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings and full-year outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Moody’s upgrades Hyundai Glovis to A3 on improved profitability
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded Hyundai Glovis Co., Ltd.'s issuer rating to A3 from Baa1 with a stable outlook, citing sustained improvements in profitability and low debt leverage. The company's operating margin improved to 7.0% in 2025 and is expected to remain stable, driven by strong performance in its pure car and truck carriers business. Hyundai Glovis's financial health is further supported by a low adjusted debt/EBITDA ratio and a net cash position of approximately KRW0.5 trillion, alongside stable operational demand from Hyundai Motor group affiliates.

feed.title: Kyowa Kirin Regains Global Rights To Atopic Dermatitis Drug After Amgen Collaboration Ends
feed.summary: Kyowa Kirin and Amgen have terminated their collaboration on the atopic dermatitis drug rocatinlimab, leading Kyowa Kirin to regain global rights for development and commercialization. Amgen's decision stems from a strategic portfolio prioritization, despite having paid an initial $400 million in 2021. Analysts note that the timing was surprising given the completed Phase 3 program, and diminished enthusiasm for the OX40 target due to efficacy and toxicity concerns for rocatinlimab.

feed.title: RBC Capital Adjusts United Rentals (URI) Price Target to $1041
feed.summary: RBC Capital has lowered its price target for United Rentals (URI) from $1123 to $1041, a 7.30% decrease, while maintaining an 'Outperform' rating. This adjustment comes amidst varied analyst perspectives, with other firms like Wells Fargo and Citigroup recently raising their targets. United Rentals, the world's largest equipment rental company, holds a strong position in a fragmented market, and the average analyst target price for the stock is $970.76, implying a 23.83% upside.

feed.title: PayPal Plunges 23.3% in 3 Months: Time to Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
feed.summary: PayPal's stock has plunged 23.3% in the last three months due to macroeconomic uncertainty, increased competition, and tariff-related pressures. Despite strategic transformations into a broader commerce platform, including plans for PayPal Bank and investments in AI-driven "agentic commerce," the company faces challenges like slowing growth, increased operating expenses, and rising fraud incidents. While the stock's valuation appears cheap compared to industry averages and competitors, Zacks Investment Research gives it a 'Sell' rating due to negative estimate revisions and ongoing risks, advising caution despite the potential for long-term recovery.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) Price Target Raised at Piper Sandler
feed.summary: Piper Sandler analyst Mark Lear raised the firm's price target on Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY) from $46 to $47, maintaining a 'Neutral' rating and indicating an almost 5% upside. This revision anticipates strong Q4 2025 earnings from gas operators and increasing demand for LNG into 2026. BofA also increased its price target for OXY from $44 to $45, reflecting updated targets for integrated, refining, and midstream stocks.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) Price Target Raised at Piper Sandler
feed.summary: Piper Sandler analyst Mark Lear raised the price target for Occidental Petroleum Corporation (OXY) from $46 to $47, maintaining a 'Neutral' rating. This comes after BofA also increased its price target for OXY from $44 to $45. Both revisions reflect expectations of strong performance in the energy sector, particularly for gas operators, as demand for LNG is projected to rise in 2026.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share at $355
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), recently purchased 1 share of TPL stock for $355.41, increasing its direct ownership to 3,487,702 shares. This transaction follows TPL's third-quarter earnings miss in 2025, a strategic partnership with Bolt Data & Energy, and the announcement of a three-for-one stock split effective December 22, 2025. KeyBanc also initiated coverage on TPL with an Overweight rating due to its extensive land and royalty holdings in Texas.

feed.title: The Truth About Carrier Global Corp: Is This ‘Boring’ Stock Actually a Silent Money Machine?
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corp. is presented as a "boring" but potentially lucrative investment. The company, a climate control heavyweight in heating, cooling, and building systems, is highlighted for its steady growth and significant role in climate tech, smart homes, and energy efficiency, despite not being a social media sensation. The article suggests that while it may not offer quick viral spikes, it holds strong potential for long-term investors interested in stable, impactful companies within the climate and infrastructure sectors.

feed.title: Uber Technologies Options Trading: A Deep Dive into Market Sentiment
feed.summary: Financial giants are making conspicuous bearish moves on Uber Technologies according to an analysis of 36 unusual options trades, with 61% showing bearish tendencies. Major market movers are focusing on a price band between $62.5 and $105.0 for Uber, and the stock is currently trading at $79.93, down 2.16%. Analysts have an average target price of $113.2 for Uber and several have recently reiterated or lowered their ratings.

feed.title: FISERV INVESTIGATION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is Investigating Fiserv, Inc. on Behalf of Long-Term Stockholders and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is investigating potential claims against Fiserv, Inc. (NASDAQ:FISV) on behalf of long-term stockholders. This follows a class action complaint filed on November 4, 2025, alleging that Fiserv made misleading statements and omissions regarding its initiatives and projects during the Class Period of July 23, 2025, to October 29, 2025. The firm encourages affected investors to contact them to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: Are Wall Street Analysts Predicting Baxter International Stock Will Climb or Sink?
feed.summary: Shares of Baxter International (BAX) have underperformed the broader market, decreasing by 40.2% over the past 52 weeks despite a recent 3.5% YTD increase. The company missed Q3 2025 revenue forecasts and cut its full-year EPS guidance due to hurricane disruptions and issues with its infusion pump portfolio. Despite these challenges, analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating on BAX, with one analyst recently cutting the price target to $30 while maintaining an "Overweight" rating.

feed.title: Roche Settles Perjata Patent Suit on Organon, Henlius Biosimilar
feed.summary: Roche, through its subsidiaries Genentech Inc. and Hoffmann-La Roche Inc., has settled a patent infringement lawsuit concerning the breast-cancer drug Perjeta with Shanghai Henlius Biotech Inc. and Organon & Co. The agreement, filed in federal court, leads to the dismissal of all claims and counterclaims, effectively ending the litigation over the biosimilar version of the drug. No specific details about the settlement terms were disclosed.

feed.title: nLight (NASDAQ:LASR) Reaches New 1-Year High - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: nLight (NASDAQ:LASR) recently hit a new 52-week high of $48.66, with a final trade at $46.67. Analyst sentiment is mixed, with an average target price of $38.43, though some firms have raised their targets. Insider selling has occurred recently, but institutional ownership remains high, and the company demonstrates strong liquidity despite a negative P/E ratio.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Jumps into World Cup Campaign with Anthemic Launch Film
feed.summary: Coca-Cola has launched its FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with an anthemic film titled 'Bubbling Up', introducing a new brand platform 'Feel It All'. The campaign, which is the first of three films, aims to capture the emotional rollercoaster of football and refresh fans through every high and low. It features a reimagining of Van Halen's 'Jump' performed by global stars and will be accompanied by a custom Panini sticker collection.

feed.title: Hubbell Incorporated Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend Payable on March 16, 2026
feed.summary: Hubbell Incorporated's Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share on the company's common stock. The dividend will be paid on March 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026. This announcement highlights Hubbell's continued commitment to returning value to its shareholders through regular dividend payouts.

feed.title: P&G Hygiene & Health Care Declares Rs 195 Interim Dividend, Details TDS Rules for FY 2025-26
feed.summary: P&G Hygiene & Health Care has declared an interim dividend of Rs 195 per share for FY 2025-26, which includes a special dividend of Rs 25. The company also provided details on Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) rules for dividend income and urged shareholders to update their PAN and tax residency details to ensure correct withholding. This measure is in line with post-2020 tax rules making dividends taxable in the hands of shareholders.

feed.title: Mixed earnings results for Weyerhaeuser, Eagle Materials
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser and Eagle Materials both reported their recent and full-year financial performance, showing mixed results. Weyerhaeuser announced net earnings of $74 million in Q4 2025 and $324 million for the full year, while also actively divesting timberland assets and launching a new growth strategy. Eagle Materials reported $556 million in revenue and $102.9 million in net earnings, with strong demand in heavy construction materials despite a decline in its Light Materials sector.

feed.title: Celebrating Careers Built To Last at Northern Trust
feed.summary: Northern Trust celebrates its Quarter Century Club, honoring employees who have reached 25-year milestones, with a record 288 new members in 2025. The company highlights the journeys of long-serving employees Steven Krause (46 years), Catherine Duffy (36 years), and Bert Rebelo (26 years), showcasing their dedication, adaptability to technological changes, and insights on career growth within the organization. Chairman and CEO Mike O’Grady emphasizes the value of this longevity and institutional knowledge.

feed.title: Fiscal Fireworks: How Debt is Rewriting the Rules for the US and Japan
feed.summary: Rising government debt levels in the US and Japan are causing concern, with traditional safe-havens weakening and gold's rally bolstering as investors search for stability. While Japan, with a debt-to-GDP ratio of 237%, is closer to a tipping point due to structural issues and inflationary pressures, the US continues to grow with stabilizing prices. Diversification, with gold as a considered asset, remains crucial for navigating these evolving markets.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Franklin Resources beats Q1 2026 forecasts
feed.summary: Franklin Resources (BEN) significantly surpassed Q1 2026 earnings and revenue forecasts, reporting an EPS of $0.70 against an expected $0.55, and revenue of $2.37 billion compared to $1.71 billion. The company saw strong performance driven by record long-term inflows, reaching $1.68 trillion in assets under management (AUM), and is targeting over 30% operating margins by fiscal 2027. Franklin Resources' strategy emphasizes diversified investment solutions, alternative assets, and technological innovation, including an AI-driven distribution platform and blockchain initiatives for efficiency.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Bank Of Montreal (TSX:BMO) After Its 1‑Year 37.9% Surge?
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (TSX:BMO) has seen a significant 37.9% surge over the last year, leading to questions about its current valuation. An Excess Returns model suggests the stock is currently 28.2% undervalued, while a Price-to-Earnings analysis indicates it is modestly overvalued compared to industry averages and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio. The article emphasizes using "Narratives" for a more comprehensive valuation that incorporates individual assumptions about the bank's business.

feed.title: Regeneron bets added cholesterol benefit will help its obesity drug stand out
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals believes its experimental weight-loss drug, olatorepatide, can gain an edge in the competitive obesity market due to its additional cholesterol-lowering benefits. The company plans late-stage trials for obesity and studies evaluating it in combination with its cholesterol drug, Praluent, to target a large population suffering from both obesity and hyperlipidemia. This strategy aims to differentiate Regeneron's offering from current weight-loss drugs that do not significantly lower bad cholesterol.

feed.title: Roth Capital Adjusts Hartford Insurance Group PT to $135 From $120, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Roth Capital has increased its price target for Hartford Insurance Group (HIG) to $135 from $120, while continuing to hold a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on the company. The Hartford Financial Services Group operates in various insurance segments including business non-life, group, and individual non-life insurance, along with mutual fund management.

feed.title: 3 Big Bank Stocks to Sell Right Now
feed.summary: Despite a strong performance in 2025, big U.S. bank stocks like Bank of America, PNC Financial Services, and Wells Fargo are facing headwinds in 2026. Factors such as potential credit card rate caps, peaked net interest margins, softening loan demand, rising expenses, and competition from BNPL services suggest that their growth may be slowing. The article recommends considering selling these specific bank stocks due to valuation concerns, capital adequacy issues, and regulatory pressure.

feed.title: Wells Fargo to pay CEO Scharf $40M for 2025
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's board has approved $40 million in compensation for CEO Charlie Scharf for 2025, a significant increase from his previous earnings. This raise reflects the bank's assessment of his performance, particularly the resolution of seven consent orders, including the lifting of a $1.95 trillion asset cap by the Federal Reserve. The bank also cited improved financial metrics and strategic investments under his leadership as justifications for the substantial compensation bump.

feed.title: 10 Materials Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies significant "whale" options trading activity across 10 materials stocks, including Alamos Gold (NYSE:AGI) and Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX). It details various call and put option trades with specific expiration dates, strike prices, and sentiment (bearish or bullish) for each company. The report serves as a tool for traders to spot potential opportunities based on large-scale market movements.

feed.title: Hecla Mining (HL) Is Down 15.9% After Cutting 2026 Output Outlook And Selling Casa Berardi – Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: Hecla Mining (HL) shares fell 15.9% after the company announced lower 2026 production guidance, despite strong 2025 output, and sold its Casa Berardi operation for up to US$593 million. This strategic shift emphasizes core silver assets while nearly doubling exploration spending, though the market is currently reassessing the company's investment narrative due to the grade pressure and asset sale. Investors should consider differing fair value estimates and execution risks related to Hecla's concentrated silver portfolio.

feed.title: The Truth About Roper Technologies Inc: Why Wall Street Is Quietly Obsessed
feed.summary: Roper Technologies Inc (ROPO) is gaining quiet obsession from Wall Street and increasing attention from retail investors due to its consistent, high-margin returns. The company operates as a diversified software and tech-enabled products firm, focusing on acquiring and scaling niche, mission-critical software businesses. Despite not being a "sexy" stock, Roper's predictable cash flow and recurring revenue streams make it an attractive long-term investment for those seeking quality and consistency over viral market trends.

feed.title: Resmed Q2: Mask sales surge as new products, resupply gain traction
feed.summary: Resmed reported strong mask sales growth of 16% in Q2 F26 for the U.S., Canada, and Latin America, driven by new product launches like the AirFit F30i Comfort mask and effective resupply strategies. The company also saw an 8% increase in device revenue in the U.S., benefiting from the absence of competitive bidding pressures. Resmed is expanding its distribution network and noted that patients with combined GLP-1 and CPAP prescriptions are more likely to engage in resupply events.

feed.title: Analyzing Kontoor Brands’ Strategic Direction Ahead of Earnings
feed.summary: Ahead of its February earnings report, investors are scrutinizing Kontoor Brands Inc.'s strategic execution, particularly its capital allocation and free cash flow generation. The company, which owns Wrangler and Lee brands, is focusing on targeted innovations like an accessory collection by Lee to enhance brand relevance in a challenging retail environment. The upcoming report will be crucial for evaluating the company's operational strength and market valuation.

feed.title: GSI Technology: Fiscal Third Quarter Earnings Overview
feed.summary: GSI Technology Inc. reported a net loss of $3 million for its fiscal third quarter, resulting in a loss of $0.09 per share. The Sunnyvale, California-based semiconductor manufacturer achieved revenues of $6.1 million during this period.

feed.title: Analyzing Extra Space Storage’s Upcoming Financial Report
feed.summary: Extra Space Storage (US30225T1025) is preparing to release its Q4 and full fiscal year 2025 financial results in mid-February, with the report due on February 19, 2026, after market close, and an earnings call the next day. Investors will focus on occupancy rates, rental prices, and especially same-store net operating income (NOI) to gauge the company's efficiency and pricing power. Beyond the financial figures, the market will intently scrutinize management's strategic outlook, capital allocation plans, and guidance for navigating the challenging real estate sector.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Q4 2025 slides: Mixed results amid strategic transition to 2030 growth plan
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) reported mixed Q4 2025 results, with GAAP earnings beating expectations due to special items, while core performance showed declines in Adjusted EBITDA and sales. Despite ongoing market challenges, the company launched a new strategy targeting $1.5 billion in incremental Adjusted EBITDA by 2030, building on past strategic successes like timberland acquisitions and operational improvements. Investors will monitor Weyerhaeuser's ability to execute its long-term growth plan and maintain shareholder returns amid current headwinds.

feed.title: Coastal Virginia Offshore Project Costs Increases to $11.5b
feed.summary: Dominion Energy announced an increase in the total project costs for the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project to approximately $11.5 billion, up from $11.2 billion. This rise is attributed to the temporary suspension of work by the U.S. government and tariffs. The project, now expected to be completed in early 2027, has already seen nearly $9 billion invested and aims to power over 600,000 homes.

feed.title: Jack Henry (JKHY) Moves to Buy: Rationale Behind the Upgrade
feed.summary: Jack Henry (JKHY) has been upgraded to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), reflecting an upward trend in its earnings estimates. This rating change is significant because a company's changing earnings picture is a powerful force impacting stock prices, driven by the actions of institutional investors. The Zacks Rank system, which correlates earnings estimate revisions with stock movements, suggests that Jack Henry's improved earnings outlook could lead to increased buying pressure and a higher stock price.

feed.title: Newmont (NEM) Stock Slides As Warsh Fed Pick Triggers Sharp Gold Selloff
feed.summary: Newmont (NEM) shares are down significantly after Donald Trump nominated Kevin Warsh as the next Fed chair, a move perceived as less friendly to ultra-easy monetary policy. This news triggered a sharp selloff in gold, impacting miners like Newmont, whose profits are highly leveraged to gold prices. Investors are advised to monitor Fed commentary from Warsh and gold's stability.

feed.title: CMS Energy’s 2025 Results to Spotlight Infrastructure Strategy and 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: CMS Energy is set to release its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial report on February 5, 2026, where investors will focus on the company's capital expenditure plans and 2026 EPS guidance. The utility has a $20 billion capital investment program through 2029 for grid modernization and clean energy expansion, aiming to increase solar and wind capacity significantly. The report will provide insight into the company's progress on these initiatives and its financial outlook.

feed.title: COIN vs. ICE: Which Financial Markets Stock is the Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article compares Coinbase (COIN) and Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) as financial markets stocks for long-term growth, analyzing their strategies, strengths, and market positioning. Coinbase aims to become an "everything exchange" capitalizing on crypto adoption and President Trump's pro-crypto stance, while ICE focuses on its diverse portfolio, risk management services, and strong balance sheet within traditional financial markets and the digital mortgage sector. While Coinbase faces margin pressures and market volatility, its expanding ecosystem is promising; ICE, despite higher operating expenses, benefits from strategic acquisitions and a strong data services platform.

feed.title: Tesla is killing the Model S and Model X to build Optimus robots — and people have thoughts
feed.summary: Tesla is reportedly discontinuing production of its Model S and Model X luxury vehicles to focus on building Optimus humanoid robots, a shift that has generated significant online debate. This move signifies Tesla's pivot from being solely an electric vehicle manufacturer to a broader tech and automation company, focusing on artificial intelligence and robotics. The decision raises questions among the public and investors about the future of Tesla's automotive lineup, long-term support for existing owners, and the potential risks and rewards of betting on unproven robot technology.

feed.title: FICO Recognized in the 2026 Gartner® Critical Capabilities Report for Decision Intelligence Platforms
feed.summary: FICO has been recognized in the 2026 Gartner® Critical Capabilities for Decision Intelligence Platforms report, ranking 2nd across all evaluated Use Cases: Decision Analysis, Decision Engineering, Decision Science, and Decision Stewardship. This recognition validates FICO Platform's strategy in meeting current and future challenges with agility, especially for decision engineering through scalable decision services and AI capabilities. The report follows FICO's recent designation as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Decision Intelligence Platforms.

feed.title: Wells Fargo launches internal proxy voting service
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's investment and wealth management arm has launched an internal proxy voting system, reducing its reliance on third-party advisers. This makes Wells Fargo the second major asset manager to create a proprietary service for proxy voting, following JPMorgan Chase's recent announcement of an AI-powered tool. The move comes amidst an executive order by former President Donald Trump targeting proxy advisory firms like ISS and Glass Lewis.

feed.title: Dover Analysts Boost Their Forecasts Following Better-Than-Expected Q4 Results
feed.summary: Dover Corporation reported better-than-expected Q4 results, with revenue of $2.099 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.51, surpassing consensus estimates. The company anticipates adjusted EPS between $10.45 and $10.65 for 2026, with sales projected between $8.498 billion and $8.656 billion. Following these strong results, analysts from Wells Fargo and RBC Capital maintained their ratings and raised their price targets for Dover.

feed.title: Celebrate IU's big win with Coca-Cola and Upland Brewing Co.'s commemorative cans
feed.summary: Indiana University fans can celebrate the Hoosiers' national championship win with commemorative cans from Coca-Cola and Upland Brewing Co. Upland Brewing Co. will release a limited supply of 8,000 six-packs of their Hoosier Gameday Lager in mid-February, while Coca-Cola Consolidated will offer individual commemorative cans featuring the 2025 championship logo in Bloomington and Indianapolis starting the week of February 3rd.

feed.title: Amgen gives up on its once-prized eczema drug
feed.summary: Amgen has decided to terminate its collaboration with Kyowa Kirin for the eczema drug rocatinlimab, returning the rights to Kyowa Kirin. This decision comes more than a year after disappointing Phase 3 trial results, which, while achieving primary goals, showed rocatinlimab was not superior to Dupixent and had a notable side effect profile, including fever and chills. Kyowa Kirin remains confident in the drug's potential and plans to seek regulatory approvals despite Amgen's withdrawal.
